rules.name: No direct console IO in shipping libraries
rules.reason: Shipping libraries must not write to process-global console streams.
rules.remediation: Route diagnostics through explicit logging, audit, or caller-owned output abstractions.

rules.name: No raw process spawning in shipping runtime code

allowedIn.reason: The opt-in TrustedWorker evaluator owns the reviewed disposable child-process boundary and enforces host-configured time and memory limits around it.

rules.reason: Process spawning crosses a host security boundary and must be explicit and reviewable.
rules.remediation: Move process execution to approved tooling or introduce a reviewed host abstraction.

rules.name: No direct networking outside transport layers

allowedIn.reason: The TCP transport package owns direct TcpClient/TcpListener usage.
The HTTP hosting package owns reviewed outbound HTTP/DNS/socket usage for pinned safe HTTP bindings.
The sandbox type guard lists forbidden framework type names; it does not open sockets.

rules.reason: Network access must stay behind approved transport layers and kernel verifier deny-lists.
rules.remediation: Use an approved transport package or extend the reviewed transport abstraction.

rules.name: No nondeterministic time or random primitives in kernel code

allowedIn.reason: Binding audit fields measure host-call elapsed time at the sandbox boundary.
SandboxPolicy owns the nondeterministic grant clock fallback for non-deterministic policies.
SandboxContext centralizes deterministic and nondeterministic clock/random sources.
The interpreter stamps run-summary audit events at the sandbox boundary.
Safe file bindings stamp audited host filesystem operations at the runtime boundary.
Safe log bindings stamp audited host log operations at the runtime boundary.
Safe random bindings delegate to SandboxContext, which enforces deterministic seeds.
Safe time bindings delegate to SandboxContext, which enforces deterministic logical time.
The compiler cache stamps quarantined artifact paths outside deterministic sandbox execution.
The compiler emits artifact manifest evidence after deterministic plan compilation.
The verifier stamps verification evidence after all deterministic checks complete.

rules.reason: Kernel execution and validation paths must not bypass the policy-owned clock/random abstractions.
rules.remediation: Use SandboxContext, SandboxPolicy logical time, deterministic seeds, or an approved boundary wrapper.

rules.name: No raw filesystem access in kernel validation except policy-owned IO checks

allowedIn.reason: FilePolicyGrantValidator is the validation component that owns policy root filesystem checks.

rules.reason: Kernel validation should not grow ad hoc filesystem probes outside the policy grant validator.
rules.remediation: Route filesystem validation through FilePolicyGrantValidator or document a new approved validator.
